summary: Decompiled several gmvsmelee routines around VS melee setup, match-end handling, CSS/SSS minor-scene data transfer, and KO-star/result bookkeeping. The PR replaced multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes, introduced MatchPlayerData, MatchTeamData, MatchEnd, MatchExitInfo, and SSSData structures, and added a gmvsmelee static header for the static MatchExitInfo storage at gm_80479D98. No review comments or PR body rationale were available in the provided slice, so the reasoning is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Implemented gm_801A5258 to update KO-star/count data for active player slots using lbTime_8000AF74 and MatchEnd.player_standings.
Implemented gm_801A52D0 as a boolean scan over MatchEnd player_standings to detect whether any slot_type == 0 active player exists.
Implemented gm_801A55C4 and gm_801A55EC_OnLoad as memzero resets of the six-byte gm_804D6730 KO-star/count storage.

Renamed gm_801A5618 parameters from generic arg names to minor_data, vs_data, and match_type, while preserving behavior: fetch CSSData with gm_801A427C, set match_type, point ko_star_counts at gm_804D6730, copy VsModeData, and call lbDvd_800174BC.
Implemented gm_801A5754 to populate SSSData from a MinorScene, copy VsModeData into it, and call gm_80167FC4.

Implemented gm_801A5AF0, marked in the header comment as Match_Exit, to process MatchExitInfo, record per-character and match result data through gm_80162574/gm_8016260C/gm_801628C4, and choose a pending minor scene based on gm_80167140.
Implemented gm_801A5EC8 to copy or merge match-end data from a MinorScene MatchExitInfo into static gm_80479D98.match_end via gm_80166CCC.
Implemented gm_801A5F00 to call gm_80177724 on a MatchEnd buffer from minor scene data and then assign gm_80479D98.match_end into it.
Added gmvsmelee.static.h containing static MatchExitInfo gm_80479D98 at address comment 479D98.
Added MatchTeamData, MatchPlayerData, MatchEnd, and MatchExitInfo definitions to gm/types.h with explicit fields, arrays, and padding to support typed decompilation of match-end structures.
Added SSSData to mn/types.h and its forward declaration to mn/forward.h.
Added MatchPlayerData, MatchEnd, and MatchExitInfo forward declarations to gm/forward.h.
Updated gm_1601.h prototypes for several helper functions used by gmvsmelee, including gm_80162574, gm_8016260C, gm_80162800, gm_801628C4, gm_80166CCC, gm_80167140, and gm_80177724.
Updated gmvsmelee.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures for the newly implemented gmvsmelee functions.
smart_moves: The PR first gave match-end memory a typed shape, then used that structure in gmvsmelee functions, reducing reliance on raw offsets and UNK_PARAMS.
Forward declarations were added for newly shared gm and menu types, avoiding heavier include coupling where only pointers are needed.
The static data symbol gm_80479D98 was isolated in gmvsmelee.static.h, matching the project pattern of keeping file-local/static data declarations separate from public headers.

The match-exit routine was expressed through semantically named locals such as match_exit_info, active_player, minor_data, vs_data, css_data, and sss_data, making scene flow and data ownership easier to audit.
Function prototypes in gm_1601.h were tightened only where the diff showed concrete uses, allowing downstream gmvsmelee code to call helper routines with typed MatchEnd pointers and scalar arguments.
The implementation preserves explicit six-entry loops over player standings, which is useful evidence for the fixed match player slot count used by these structures.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled routine repeatedly indexes the same large match-end buffer, defining a partial struct with named fields and padding can unlock several adjacent functions at once.

Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in headers should be paired with callsite evidence; this PR demonstrates deriving prototypes from concrete gmvsmelee uses such as MatchEnd* arguments and u8/u32 scalar bookkeeping calls.

MinorScene payload accessors such as gm_801A427C and gm_801A4284 can return different logical data types depending on scene context; typed locals in each function clarify whether the payload is CSSData, SSSData, MatchEnd, or MatchExitInfo.
Static game-mode state that is not public API belongs in a static header or file-local declaration, not in the exported gmvsmelee.h interface.

For match-end structures, preserve unknown bytes and large padding ranges when field meaning is not yet known; the added MatchEnd and MatchPlayerData structs keep named fields only where evidence exists and pad the rest.

Boolean helper decompilation can often be simplified to explicit active-slot scans, but the exact condition should remain tied to observed slot_type values rather than guessed enum names unless those enums exist.

Data-transfer scene hooks commonly have simple copy semantics: CSS/SSS setup copies VsModeData into scene-specific data, while match-exit hooks copy MatchEnd data between scene payloads and static storage.
naming_conventions: New match-end structs use PascalCase names: MatchTeamData, MatchPlayerData, MatchEnd, MatchExitInfo.
Known callback-style functions keep suffixes such as OnInit, OnLoad, and OnUnload in gmvsmelee.h.
Locals were renamed from arg0/arg1/arg2 to role-based snake_case names such as minor_data, vs_data, match_type, css_data, sss_data, exit_info, match_exit_info, active_player, and end_info.
Unknown struct fields retain offset-based names such as x0, x5, xC, x20, x24, _x448, pad_x44C, and pad_x186C rather than speculative semantic names.
The header comment for gm_801A5AF0 adds a tentative semantic marker, '// Match_Exit', while preserving the address-based symbol name.

Forward declarations follow the project typedef pattern: typedef struct MatchPlayerData MatchPlayerData; typedef struct MatchEnd MatchEnd; typedef struct MatchExitInfo MatchExitInfo; typedef struct SSSData SSSData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used fixed-size loops from i = 0 to i < 6 for player_standings and winners-related match data, matching the six-player/slot layout present in the structures.
Used explicit slot_type == 0 checks as the active-player predicate in gm_801A5258, gm_801A52D0, and gm_801A5AF0.
Kept casts where likely needed for matching, such as casting n_ko[i] to u32 before lbTime_8000AF74 and dividing frame_count by 60 before passing to gm_801628C4.
Preserved an explicit active_player variable and break-based loop in gm_801A5AF0 instead of collapsing the logic into a helper, which may help maintain compiler-compatible control flow.
Represented unknown large tail regions in MatchEnd and MatchPlayerData with padding arrays, enabling correct offsets without overnaming unverified fields.
Used direct struct assignment for VsModeData and MatchEnd copies, indicating the matching source likely performed aggregate copies rather than field-by-field assignments.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No PR body rationale was available; conclusions about intent are inferred from changed files and diff content.
follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that refine MatchEnd, MatchPlayerData, MatchTeamData, or MatchExitInfo field names and sizes.
Check whether gm_801A5360, gm_801A5680, gm_801A57A8, gm_801A583C, gm_801A5C3C, and gm_801A5F64 were subsequently decompiled using these structs.
Look for later review or cleanup around gmvsmelee.static.h and whether static MatchExitInfo gm_80479D98 remained file-local or moved.
Search for enum definitions or constants that replace slot_type == 0, match result fields, and active-player magic values.
Verify whether SSSData fields unk_stage, no_lras, force_stage_id, and start_game were later renamed with stronger menu-system evidence.
